question_title: How many commercial aircraft deliveries will Airbus report for March 2026 ?
summary: Airbus March 2026 commercial aircraft deliveries forecast based on 3 years of monthly delivery data, seasonal patterns, and current production context. Historical March deliveries: 61 (2023), 63 (2024), 71 (2025). Linear trend projects ~75. However, January 2026 was only 19 (slowest in a decade), indicating supply chain headwinds that may limit the normal Q1 ramp. Airbus internally targets ~900 deliveries for 2026 but hasn't confirmed. Blended Monte Carlo model using trend extrapolation, scenario analysis, and seasonal patterns produces a median around 71 with substantial uncertainty in both directions.

factors.description: Historical March trend: 61→63→71 over 2023-2025, linear extrapolation gives 75 for 2026
January 2026 was only 19 deliveries - slowest start in a decade, signaling persistent supply chain constraints
Airbus targeting ~900 deliveries for 2026 (13.5% growth), implying March should be ~77 if proportional
March is consistently strongest Q1 month (quarter-end push) with March/Jan ratios of 2.1-3.1x historically
Record backlog of 8,754 aircraft means demand is not the constraint - only production capacity limits deliveries
A320neo production ramp to 75/month targeted for 2027, gradual increase underway in 2026
Airbus missed its original 2025 target (820→790 revised), pattern of over-promising on ramp schedules

**Key findings:**
- Historical March deliveries: 61 (2023), 63 (2024), 71 (2025) — clear upward trend
- Linear trend projects ~75 for March 2026
- January 2026 was only 19 deliveries (slowest start in a decade), signaling supply chain headwinds
- Airbus targets ~900 deliveries for 2026 (up 13.5% from 793), but hasn't confirmed officially
- Record backlog of 8,754 aircraft means demand is not the constraint — production capacity is
- March is consistently the strongest Q1 month due to quarter-end push

**Central estimate:** ~71 aircraft (matching March 2025 level), with meaningful downside risk from persistent supply issues and upside from production ramp-up efforts.
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as the total number of Airbus commercial aircraft “deliveries” for March 2026, using Airbus’ official Orders & Deliveries data:
Primary source: https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/commercial-aircraft/market/orders-and-deliveries
Backup source: Airbus IR “Orders & deliveries” / monthly releases (if the above page structure changes). Question may also resolve based on  [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
background_info:
Airbus deliveries are a leading indicator for aerospace revenue recognition, supply-chain throughput, and airline fleet demand. Primary source: Airbus 'Orders and Deliveries' reports page (monthly reports that can be summed for Jan–Mar 2026), https://www.airbus.com/en/products-services/commercial-aircraft/orders-and-deliveries.
